title: apoballis grandiflora
intro: Apoballis grandiflora, also known as the golden cup flower or golden pitcher plant, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Thailand. It is known for its unique, large, golden-yellow flowers that resemble a cup or pitcher, hence its common name. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. Apoballis grandiflora is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and can be prepared into various herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures.
